name: Dark mode toggle in the dashboard
description: Add a three-state (system / light / dark) theme toggle to the dashboard header and plumb Tailwind's dark: variant through the existing components so the whole UI respects it. Default is 'system' (follows prefers-color-scheme). The user's explicit choice persists in localStorage and is applied before first paint to avoid a flash of incorrect theme.
acceptance_criteria: Tailwind config is updated to darkMode: 'class' (if not already) so dark: variants are driven by a class on <html>

A small ThemeToggle component lives in web/src/components/ui/ThemeToggle.tsx with three states (system, light, dark), renders as a single button that cycles on click with a clear label/aria-label, and is placed in the top nav/header (web/src/components/layout/...) visible on every page

Selected theme persists in localStorage under the key 'theme' ('system' | 'light' | 'dark'); on page load, an inline <script> in the <head> (in web/src/app/layout.tsx) reads localStorage + prefers-color-scheme and sets the html class before hydration so there is no flash of incorrect theme

All Card, Badge, Button, ErrorMessage, Spinner, layout, and page components render correctly in dark mode: text contrast is legible (WCAG AA), no hard-coded pure-white backgrounds leak through, map/video/chart components continue to work (Leaflet tiles, hls.js controls, canvas signal timeline). Where third-party widgets do not theme, document the compromise in a comment on that component
Changing the system theme while in 'system' mode immediately updates the UI (listen to matchMedia change)
pnpm --dir web lint, pnpm --dir web type-check, and pnpm --dir web test pass
Manually verified: no flash of incorrect theme on hard refresh, toggle cycles system -> light -> dark -> system, preference survives reload, every top-level route is readable in both modes
notes: Tailwind v4 registers the dark: variant via @custom-variant (the v4 equivalent of darkMode: 'class'). Theme classes (light/dark) are set on <html> by a small pre-hydration script in app/layout.tsx and by the ThemeToggle component. Most components already used CSS variables so they adapted automatically; the SignalTimeline canvas now reads its ink color off computed style so the playhead stays visible in light mode. OpenStreetMap tiles are intentionally left bright in both modes (documented inline).
